data.title: Pharma CEOs set to meet with Trump at White House - report
data.description:
• The CEOs of at least three pharmaceutical companies -- Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), Merck (NYSE:MRK), and Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) -- are slated to meet with President Trump at the White House next week.
  • The Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA), the main pharma industry lobbying group, will also be there, and executives from other pharmas and biotechs may also come, according to a report in Endpoints News.
  • The meeting comes on the heels of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. being sworn in as HHS secretary on Feb. 13. Some industry executives are worried about his potential impact on the industry given his prior comments on vaccines and drugs, and his calls for major reform at the U.S. FDA, not to mention the Trump administration's cuts at the NIH, which conducts biomedical research.
  • The executives may also want to discuss potential changes to Medicare drug price negotiations that are included in the Inflation Reduction Act.
  • Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Chevron set to ramp up oil exports from Venezuela to seven-year high - Bloomberg
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) plans to boost oil exports from Venezuela to a seven-year high, Bloomberg reported Friday, after a meeting between Venezuela President Maduro and Trump envoy Richard Grenell eased fears of additional sanctions.

Exports of synthetic oil from Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) Petropiar project are expected to rise by ~50% to 143K bbl/day this month, the highest since March 2018, according to preliminary port schedules compiled by Bloomberg.

Maduro is benefiting from a slow revival of Venezuela's oil industry, after his release of U.S. prisoners and decision to accept immigrants deported from the U.S. following the meeting with Grenell.

"The Maduro-Grenell meeting is promising for [state-run] Petroleos de Venezuela SA and Western oil companies operating in Venezuela, and supportive for Venezuelan production," Rapidan Energy's Fernando Ferreira told Bloomberg.

Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) ability to expand in Venezuela remains limited by sanctions, but CEO Mike Wirth has said Venezuelan oil could prove important for the U.S. if the Trump administration imposes tariffs on Canada and Mexico, which produce crude similar to Venezuela's grades.

Separately, a Chevron-led (CVX) consortium and the government of Cyprus said Friday they have reached agreement on an updated development plan for the offshore Aphrodite gas field.

The approved development and production plan includes a floating production unit and a pipeline for exporting gas to Egypt.

data.title: Agnico Eagle plans 50% Ontario production growth by 2030 supported by new projects
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Agnico Eagle Mines Limited (NYSE:AEM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Ammar Al-Joundi emphasized that 2024 was a record year for Agnico Eagle, with record annual gold production, earnings, operating cash flow, and free cash flow. He highlighted robust cost controls and strong operational performance, which resulted in delivering leverage to gold prices. The company returned almost $1 billion to shareholders in dividends and share buybacks while reducing net debt by $1.3 billion.
  • Al-Joundi noted the company's plans to achieve extraordinary production growth, including the potential for both Malartic and Detour to each produce over 1 million ounces of gold annually, placing them among the top six largest gold mines globally. He also described the Ontario platform's potential 50% production growth by 2030, driven by the Upper Beaver and Detour Underground projects.
  • CFO Jamie Porter reported record Q4 financial metrics, including $2.2 billion in revenue, $632 million in adjusted earnings, and $1.1 billion in operating cash flow. He pointed out significant reductions in net debt by year-end to $217 million and highlighted cost discipline, with total cash costs for the year at $903 per ounce, within guidance.
  • EVP Dominique Girard praised operational outperformance in Nunavut, with record throughput at Meliadine and record gold production at Meadowbank. He also discussed advancing growth projects, including Hope Bay, which aims to produce over 400,000 ounces annually, and Malartic’s potential expansion to 1 million ounces per year by the early 2030s.
  • EVP Natasha Vaz announced records in operational efficiencies across sites, including Macassa, Detour, and Fosterville, and outlined plans for mill optimization at Macassa and further development at Detour Underground and Upper Beaver to support future growth.

Outlook

  • Production guidance for 2025-2026 was revised down by 2.9% due to adjustments in mine sequencing and challenging ground conditions at Pinos Altos. However, steady production guidance for 2027 reflects the company’s ability to transition to growth projects starting in 2030.
  • Management reaffirmed its Ontario platform growth target of a 50% increase in gold production beginning in 2030, supported by Detour Underground and Upper Beaver. These projects are expected to deliver low-risk, profitable growth in a stable mining jurisdiction.
  • Hope Bay’s development timeline targets detailed project updates by the first half of 2026, incorporating exploration results from Patch 7, which has increased indicated resources significantly.

Financial Results

  • Record Q4 performance included 847,000 ounces of gold production at total cash costs of $923 per ounce and all-in sustaining costs of $1,316 per ounce. Full-year production was 3.49 million ounces, above the midpoint of guidance.
  • Full-year free cash flow reached $2.1 billion, driven by an average realized gold price of $2,384 per ounce. Sustaining costs of $1,239 per ounce were within guidance, and all-in sustaining costs remained hundreds of dollars below peers.
  • The company allocated 40% of free cash flow to shareholder returns and used the remainder to reduce net debt. Exploration spending hit record levels, reflecting a focus on long-term growth.

Q&A

  • Anita Soni, CIBC World Markets, inquired about the Hope Bay timeline and its inclusion in the growth pipeline. EVP Dominique Girard responded that the focus this year is on project scope and engineering, with detailed updates expected by early 2026.
  • Joshua Wolfson, RBC Capital Markets, asked about capital allocation for excess free cash flow. CFO Jamie Porter explained the company aims to return approximately one-third of free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks while further strengthening the balance sheet.
  • Daniel Major, UBS, questioned the projected life of Meadowbank post-2028. EVP Girard noted plans to transition to underground mining, which could sustain production of 150,000 ounces annually through 2035.
  • Lawson Winder, Bank of America Securities, asked about potential future expansion at Malartic. EVP Girard mentioned plans to explore additional projects, including a second Odyssey shaft and potential regional discoveries to maximize mill capacity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed slightly positive sentiment, praising operational results and financial metrics but seeking clarity on the timeline for growth projects and capital allocation priorities. Questions largely centered on sustaining future production and maximizing free cash flow.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing cost discipline, strong project pipelines, and shareholder returns. Statements such as "we are uniquely positioned" and "we remain determined" underscored their confidence in delivering future growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone showed a greater focus on advancing long-term projects and addressing near-term production adjustments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Production guidance for 2025-2026 was revised down this quarter, as opposed to reaffirming steady production in Q3 2024.
  • Q4 highlighted record financial results across multiple metrics, whereas Q3 focused on maintaining cost guidance and steady production.
  • Management’s tone in Q4 reflected heightened confidence in long-term growth projects like Upper Beaver and Malartic’s expansion, compared to Q3’s emphasis on operational efficiencies and sustaining margins.
  • Analysts in Q4 raised more specific questions about capital allocation and growth project timelines, signaling an increased focus on long-term strategic execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include adjusting to ground conditions at Pinos Altos, which impacted production guidance, and managing sustaining capital costs at mature mines like Pinos Altos and La India.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about balancing growth initiatives with disciplined capital allocation while maintaining strong shareholder returns.
  • Permitting uncertainties at San Nicolas and Hope Bay could delay project timelines.
  • Management plans to mitigate risks through advanced engineering, cost control, and leveraging internal expertise.

Final Takeaway

Agnico Eagle’s Q4 2024 call underscored record operational and financial performance, driven by strong gold prices, cost controls, and disciplined capital allocation. The company is well-positioned for long-term growth, with projects like Upper Beaver and Detour Underground supporting Ontario’s 50% production increase by 2030 and Malartic targeting 1 million ounces annually. While production guidance was slightly revised for 2025-2026, management remains confident in delivering shareholder value through disciplined execution and strategic investments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Engaged Capital's Q4 moves include BlackLine, Nevro exits, new stake in Chart Industries
data.description:
Engaged Capital exited its stakes in BlackLine (NASDAQ:BL) and Nevro (NYSE:NVRO) and took a new position in Chart Industries (NYSE:GTLS) in Q4 2024, among other moves.

The hedge fund, known for its investor activism, disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing for the three months ended December 31, 2024, published on Friday.

Engaged Capital sold off its holdings in financial cloud services provider BlackLine (NASDAQ:BL) and in medical device company Nevro (NYSE:NVRO). The latter has agreed to be bought by Globus Medical (GMED) for $250M.

The fund picked up 224.8K shares in cryogenic gas liquefier Chart (NYSE:GTLS).

In other moves, it raised its stake in specialty care manager Evolent Health (EVH) by 35.5% to 5.40M class A shares. The activist investor recently reached a cooperation agreement with EVH.

Engaged Capital has most notably targeted Vans sneakers maker VF Corp (VFC) in the past. Its stake in the company remained unchanged in Q4.

More on 13F filings

  • Renaissance Technologies top Q4 moves include taking in Intel, exiting Arm & upping Nike
  • Soros Capital takes new Nvidia, Chipotle stakes, exits Alibaba, Microsoft, among top Q4 moves
  • SoundHound AI, Serve, Nano-X in focus as Nvidia exits stakes; cuts Arm investment
  • Ray Dalio's Bridgewater takes new stake in Tesla, exits Micron & Goldman Sachs in Q4 moves
  • Seth Klarman's Baupost piles into Restaurant Brands stake, takes in Humana among Q4 moves

data.title: American Axle targets $6.05B revenue and $230M free cash flow in 2025 with Dowlais combination
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: American Axle & Manufacturing Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:AXL) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO David Dauch highlighted that Q4 2024 sales reached $1.4 billion, contributing to full-year sales of $6.1 billion. Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $749 million, representing 12.2% of sales, and adjusted free cash flow was $230 million. He noted that the company had achieved the high end of its adjusted EBITDA range and exceeded the midpoint of its free cash flow target.

  • The company announced a contract extension to supply power transfer units for Ford's Maverick and Bronco Sport vehicles, bolstering its core legacy driveline business. Additionally, a transformational combination with Dowlais was unveiled, aimed at creating a leading global driveline and metal-forming supplier with a combined $12 billion in revenues and $300 million in run-rate synergies.

  • CFO Chris May reported Q4 2024 sales of $1.38 billion, reflecting a year-over-year decline due to lower North American production and timing of new product launches. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $160.8 million, with adjusted EPS showing a loss of $0.06 per share. Full-year net debt ended at $2.1 billion, with a net leverage ratio of 2.8x.

Outlook

  • Management provided 2025 guidance with revenue expected to be between $5.8 billion and $6.05 billion, adjusted EBITDA ranging from $700 million to $760 million, and adjusted free cash flow projected at $200 million to $230 million. North American production is forecasted at approximately 15.1 million units.

  • R&D expenses are anticipated to decrease by $20 million year-over-year in 2025 as the company optimizes spending to align with current market demands.

  • The pending sale of the India commercial vehicle axle business is expected to be completed by mid-2025, and financial guidance reflects this timeline.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 adjusted free cash flow was $79 million, supported by operational performance, inventory reductions, and lower interest costs. For the full year, adjusted free cash flow totaled $230 million.

  • Gross profit for Q4 2024 was $154.3 million, comparable to the prior year despite lower sales. SG&A expenses, including R&D, decreased to $89 million, representing 6.4% of sales.

  • Capital expenditures for Q4 2024 were $77.6 million, and full-year net cash from operating activities reached $151.2 million. Total available liquidity at year-end stood at $1.5 billion.

Q&A

  • John Murphy, Bank of America, inquired about production mix for GM trucks and Ram HD programs. CFO Chris May explained that GM's full-size truck production is expected to range between 1.3 million and 1.4 million units for 2025. The Ram HD platform is projected to maintain relatively flat year-over-year volumes, with stronger production anticipated after Q1 2025.

  • Jake, BNP, asked about free cash flow projections. Chris May attributed favorable performance to working capital improvements, lower interest expenses, and inventory reductions. He emphasized that structural changes in working capital should sustain these cash flow levels into 2025.

  • Edison Yu, Deutsche Bank, questioned the strategic rationale for the Dowlais combination. CEO David Dauch emphasized that the deal enhances the company's size, scale, and geographic balance, reducing GM dependency from 40% to 25% and North American exposure from 73% to 54%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed curiosity about the Dowlais combination's strategic benefits and financial implications, suggesting a slightly positive sentiment toward the merger. Questions about cash flow and production dynamics indicated cautious optimism regarding operational efficiencies.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, using phrases such as "we are positioned" and "we have high conviction" to highlight growth opportunities and synergy targets. However, responses during the Q&A revealed a more measured approach when addressing production variability and market uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue decreased sequentially from $1.5 billion in Q3 2024 to $1.38 billion in Q4 2024, reflecting lower production volumes and launch timing. Adjusted EBITDA similarly declined from $174 million in Q3.

  • Q4 2024 adjusted free cash flow of $79 million marked an improvement from Q3's $75 million, supported by stronger operational performance and inventory reductions.

  • Management's tone shifted slightly toward caution in Q4 as they acknowledged short-term production challenges, contrasting with the more optimistic outlook conveyed in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited variability in North American production volumes and launch scheduling as key challenges. They also highlighted potential impacts from tariffs and interest rates, which could influence consumer demand and OEM production.

  • To mitigate these risks, the company emphasized operational efficiency, cost controls, and leveraging its diversified product portfolio to adapt to evolving market conditions.

Final Takeaway

American Axle reported solid 2024 financial results, achieving the high end of its adjusted EBITDA target and exceeding free cash flow expectations. The company's 2025 guidance reflects a cautious yet optimistic outlook, with revenue expected to reach up to $6.05 billion and significant synergies anticipated from the Dowlais combination. Management remains focused on securing its core driveline business, optimizing R&D spending, and navigating market uncertainties to drive profitability and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trump says he wouldn't mind a Japanese co. taking a minority stake in US Steel
data.description:
President Donald Trump said he wouldn't mind if a Japanese firm acquired a minority stake in US Steel (NYSE:X).

Trumps made the comments in the Oval Office, according to a Bloomberg account of the event.

Trump's latest comments come after Trump on Thursday suggested that tariffs will save US Steel (NYSE:X), and it doesn't need to do a deal with Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY).

Last Friday, Trump said that Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) was considering investing in US Steel (X), but won't purchase the entire company.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) will keep its plan to purchase US Steel (X) and will notify the US government of its intentions. Although Trump has voiced his opposition to the $14 billion purchase of US Steel (X), Nippon Steel will not compromise, according to a TV Tokyo report on Thursday.

Shares of US Steel (X) ticked lower by ~1% on Friday.

data.title: Usiminas targets Q1 2025 domestic steel sales growth and projects 2.5% GDP growth for Brazil
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Usinas Siderúrgicas de Minas Gerais S.A. (OTCPK:USNZY) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Marcelo Chara, CEO, highlighted operational advances in 2024, achieving the second-largest crude steel production volume since 2015, with an 11% reduction in cost of sales per ton. The adjusted consolidated EBITDA reached BRL2.6 billion, with the steel unit achieving a 31% EBITDA growth year-over-year. He emphasized investments in repairing the coke and PCI plants, which are expected to reduce fuel costs and CO2 emissions.
  • Chara noted a 9.8% increase in flat steel demand to 15.7 million tons in 2024, driven by strong performance in the automotive, home appliances, and industrial sectors. He underscored the urgency of trade defense measures to combat unfair competition from subsidized imports, particularly from China.
  • Thiago Rodrigues, CFO, reported a net leverage ratio of 0.58 times at year-end 2024 and highlighted a $500 million debt issuance completed in January 2025 to extend debt maturities. Domestic steel sales rose 8% year-over-year, offsetting declines in mining production and sales.
  • The company’s Q4 2024 adjusted EBITDA grew 22% quarter-over-quarter to BRL528 million, supported by improved sales volumes in the steel segment.

Outlook

  • For Q1 2025, the company expects increased domestic steel sales, a better product mix, and slightly improved net revenue per ton due to price transfers in industrial contracts. Sales costs are projected to decrease further through continuous operational efficiency gains.
  • Chara projected a 2.5% GDP growth for Brazil in 2025, supported by sectors like home appliances, automobiles, and household essentials, while cautioning that high interest rates may slow growth.
  • Mining unit sales are anticipated to remain stable, with consolidated improvements expected by Q3 2025.

Financial Results

  • Domestic steel sales increased by 8% year-over-year, while total steel sales grew by 6%. Cost of goods sold dropped 11%, significantly boosting results despite weaker market prices. Adjusted EBITDA for the steel segment was BRL366 million for Q4 2024, with a stable margin of 6.3%.
  • Mining revenues remained stable at BRL767 million for Q4 2024 despite lower sales volumes, supported by favorable iron ore pricing and reduced freight costs. The unit’s EBITDA reached BRL154 million, driven by a 14 percentage point margin increase.
  • The company ended 2024 with a cash balance of BRL6 billion and a CapEx of BRL1.1 billion, aligned with guidance. CapEx for 2025 is projected between BRL1.4 billion and BRL1.6 billion, focusing on sustainability and operational improvements.

Q&A

  • Daniel Sasson, Itaú BBA, asked about cost reductions and efficiency gains. CFO Thiago Rodrigues explained that Q1 2025 cost reductions will primarily stem from operational efficiencies rather than significant exchange rate impacts.
  • Carlos de Alba, Morgan Stanley, inquired about working capital. Rodrigues clarified that working capital levels remain stable and are tied to sales volume fluctuations, with no major structural changes expected in 2025.
  • Miguel Homes, Vice President of Sales, addressed export strategies, noting a focus on serving the domestic market and regional exports, particularly to Argentina. Export volumes to non-regional markets are expected to decline in Q1 2025, with a better product mix potentially improving average prices.
  • Analysts raised concerns about CapEx delays. Rodrigues attributed these to the complexity of repairing the coke plant while maintaining operations, emphasizing that all approved investments are on track.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed slight concerns regarding cost pressures, macroeconomic uncertainties, and CapEx delays. Their tone was slightly negative, emphasizing challenges in global trade and domestic market conditions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, highlighting the success of operational efficiencies, strategic investments, and resilience in addressing external challenges. CEO Marcelo Chara emphasized the company’s preparedness to navigate fluctuating market conditions.
  • During the Q&A, management adopted a more defensive tone, particularly on trade defense measures and macroeconomic risks, while reiterating confidence in long-term strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Without the previous quarter's transcript, direct comparisons are unavailable. However, management reiterated its focus on operational efficiency gains, trade defense measures, and improving cost structures.
  • Analysts shifted their focus toward uncertainties in macroeconomic conditions and their potential impact on demand, reflecting cautious sentiment for 2025.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted challenges from subsidized imports, particularly from China, which have disrupted the domestic market. Chara emphasized the need for stronger trade defense measures to protect local industries.
  • Analysts raised concerns about high interest rates, macroeconomic slowdowns, and sustaining recent cost reductions.
  • Delays in key projects, including coke plant repairs and PCI plant upgrades, were noted but attributed to operational complexities.

Final Takeaway

Usiminas ended 2024 with strong operational efficiency gains and cost reductions, despite challenges from global market dynamics and unfair competition. Management remains optimistic about domestic steel sales growth in 2025, supported by strategic investments and trade defense advocacy. However, macroeconomic uncertainties and high interest rates pose potential risks to growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Phathom spikes as Cantor Fitzgerald bullish on IP claims
data.description:
Phathom Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:PHAT) traded higher on Friday after Cantor Fitzgerald initiated its coverage with an overweight recommendation despite a recent selloff attributed to the company's IP rights-related risk.

Cantor analyst Kristen Kluska argued that since early November, Phathom (NASDAQ:PHAT) had lost 67% compared to the 13% decline in the broader biotech space as represented by the SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI).

Kluska noted that “concerns around Phathom's intellectual property,” related to its heartburn medication Voquezna (vonoprazan) have led to the selloff and added, “We acknowledge this remains a risk on the stock.”

However, the analyst argued that in her base case, assuming a patent term extension to 2030 and only a 50% probability of success for market exclusivity in 2031-2032, the stock “still gives significant upside from the current valuation.”

“Our checks support that at minimum, PHAT has strong formulation and compound patents that provide protection into 2030, something we believe the Street is missing," Kluska wrote with a $23 per share target on the stock.

data.title: Barrick Gold considers redomiciling to U.S., CEO Bristow says
data.description:
Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) is weighing a relocation of its corporate domicile from Canada to the U.S., as a move could attract a broader base of investors, CEO Mark Bristow told The Globe and Mail on Friday.

A relocation also could position Barrick (NYSE:GOLD) for inclusion in the S&P 500, which would trigger automatic purchases from many mutual funds and ETFs that track the index, the CEO said.

Barrick (GOLD) has a strong operational presence in the U.S.: The Nevada Gold Mines joint venture with Newmont is the company's largest operation and the world's biggest gold-producing complex, expected to produce up to 1.7M attributable gold oz this year, accounting for nearly half of Barrick’s total output; the Goldrush project in Nevada, which is anticipated to produce more than 400K oz/year by 2028; and the Fourmile gold project, also in Nevada.

Bristow also suggested the Trump administration could facilitate a move to the U.S. under its “America First” agenda.

Tax regulations make relocating to the U.S. is a complicated and costly process, so a faster alternative could be acquiring a major U.S. company, but Bristow says there are few attractive acquisition targets.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Apple product event, Walmart earnings, Sandisk debut, and Visa's Investor Day
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Monday - February 17

  • All day - The U.S. markets will be closed for observance of the President's Day holiday.
  • All day - The Chicago Auto Show will wrap up with interactive elements and activation events, including test drives. Electric vehicle makers will have a heavy presence at the auto show, including Polestar (PSNY) with the Polestar 3, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) with the R1T and R1 models, VinFast Automotive (VFS) with the VF8, Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) with the ID.4 and ID.Buzz models, and a rare auto show appearance for Tesla (TSLA) with the Cybertruck on display.

Tuesday - February 18

  • Volatility watch - MicroCloud Hologram (HOLO) and Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (FFIE) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Medical Properties Trust (MPW) and Kohl's (KSS) head into the new week with a high level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Firefly Neuroscience (AIFF), Anteris Technologies Global (AVR), and Triumph Group (TGI). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Venture Global (VG), Columbus McKinnon (CMCO), and Brazil Potash (GRO).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Arista Networks (ANET), Medtronic (MDT), and Baidu (BIDU). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Bumble (BMBL) and Iqiyi (IQ) after their reports.
  • All day - Notable investor events include the Jacobs Solutions' (J) Investor Day event.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from Kraft Heinz (KHC), Hershey (HSY), General Mills (GIS), and Coca-Cola (KO).
  • All day - Shareholders with Discover Financial (DFS) and Capital One (COF) will vote on the pending mega-merger between the banking giants.
  • All day - The three-day CoinDesk Consensus Hong Kong event will begin. The crypto conference marks the first expansion of the Consensus event series beyond North America.
  • All day - The quiet period ends on Venture Global (VG) to free up analysts to issue ratings.
  • 10:20 a.m. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly speaks and participates in a moderated discussion before the 2025 Conference for Community Bankers hosted by the American Bankers Association.

Wednesday - February 19

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Analog Devices (ADI), Toast (TOST), and HSBC (HSBC). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Carvana (CVNA) and Etsy (ETSY) after their reports.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from PepsiCo (PEP), Altria (MO), Molson Coors (TAP), and Philip Morris International (PM).
  • All day - Notable investor events include BrightView Holdings' (BV) Investor Day and Lam Research's (LRCX) Investor Day.
  • All day - Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) is expected to start trading after the company prices its IPO.
  • 12:00 p.m. Apple (AAPL) will announce the launch of a new product at a launch event. The announcement is expected to be conducted through a press release and promotional materials rather than a live-streamed keynote. A fourth-generation iPhone SE is widely expected to be featured at the event, although there is speculation about a new MacBook Air, new iPad Air, or a surprise reveal.
  • 7:30 a.m. JetBlue Airways Corporation (JBLU) will participate in a fireside chat at the Barclays 42nd Annual Industrial Select Conference.
  • 11:35 a.m United Airlines (UAL) will present at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Open Market Committee will issue minutes of its latest meeting.

Thursday - February 20

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Walmart (WMT), Alibaba (BABA), Booking Holdings (BKNG), MercadoLibre (MELI), VICI Properties (VICI), and Block (XYZ). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Rivian Automotive (RIVN) and Unity Software (U) after their reports are released.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from Procter & Gamble (PG), US Foods (USFD), Diageo (DEO), and Clorox (CLX).
  • 8:00 a.m Walmart (WMT) will hold its earnings conference call. The retail giant's business updates have impacted shares of Target (TGT), Costco (COST), Dollar General (DG), Dollar Tree (DLTR), and Kroger (KR) in the past. Major suppliers with a strong post-earnings trading correlation with Walmart include Reynolds Consumer Products (REYN), TreeHouse Foods (THS), and Flower Foods (FLO).
  • 8:30 a.m Mobileye Global (MBLY) will present at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference.
  • 8:30 a.m The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.
  • 8:40 a.m Boeing (BA) CEO Kelly Ortberg will speak at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference.
  • 10:00 a.m Visa (V) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • 12:05 p.m St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak and participate in a moderated conversation on the U.S. economy and monetary policy before the Economic Club of New York.

Friday - February 21

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Vipshop (VIPS) and United States Cellular (USM).
  • All day - Global flash PMI reports will pour in.
  • All day - Sandisk (SDK) will start trading after being spun off from Western Digital (WDC). The business split will create two independent, publicly traded companies focused on different aspects of data storage technology.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from Celsius Holdings (CELH) and Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY).
  • All day - Apimeds Pharmaceuticals (APUS) is expected to start trading after the company prices its IPO.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Existing Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.
